items.text: Hi Jordan, thanks for volunteering as a campus tour guide! Your first shift is this Friday at 10 a.m. by the Visitor Center. Please pick up your guide badge from the front desk beforehand; there is a $5 refundable deposit. Wear comfortable shoes. Best, Maria

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Jordan do before the shift starts?

options.A: Collect a guide badge from the front desk
options.C: Email the Visitor Center for directions
options.D: Attend a training session on Thursday

questions.explanation: The email asks Jordan to 'pick up your guide badge from the front desk beforehand.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the deposit?

options.A: It pays for the cost of the tour
options.B: It must be paid only in cash
options.C: It can be returned after the badge is given back
options.D: It rises for later volunteer shifts

questions.explanation: Because the deposit is described as 'refundable,' it can be returned once the badge is returned.

items.text: FREE FLU SHOTS — no appointment needed! Student Health Center, Room 120. Wednesday, October 8, from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. Bring your student ID and your insurance card. Walk-ins are welcome all day. Protect yourself and your classmates this season!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, where will the flu shots be given?

options.B: In Room 120 of the Student Health Center

questions.explanation: The flyer lists the location as 'Student Health Center, Room 120.'

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what should students bring to the clinic?

options.A: A student ID and an insurance card

questions.explanation: The flyer says to 'bring your student ID and your insurance card.'

items.text: Hi Sam, this confirms your tutoring appointment with the Writing Center. We will meet on Tuesday at 2 p.m. in Room 305, Henley Hall. Please bring a printed draft of your essay so we can review it together. Let me know if you need to reschedule. Thanks, Dana

questions.stem: According to the email, where will the appointment take place?

options.A: In the main library reading room
options.B: At the front desk of Henley Hall

questions.explanation: The email states the meeting will be 'in Room 305, Henley Hall.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the tutoring session?

options.A: It will be conducted entirely online
options.B: Sam is expected to have written something already
options.C: Sam must pay a fee before attending
options.D: The meeting will continue for several hours

questions.explanation: Since Sam must bring 'a printed draft of your essay,' he is expected to have written a draft beforehand.

items.text: Hi Lee, your campus parking permit is ready for pickup at the Transportation Office in Building C. Office hours are weekdays from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m. Please bring $20 and your vehicle registration. Permits not collected by Friday will be released to the waitlist. Regards, Omar

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Lee bring to pick up the permit?

options.B: A photo for the permit card
options.C: Twenty dollars and a vehicle registration
options.D: A signed letter from an advisor

questions.explanation: The email asks Lee to 'bring $20 and your vehicle registration.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about permits not collected by Friday?

options.A: They will be mailed to the student
options.B: They can be picked up the next month
options.D: They may be given to another person

questions.explanation: Since uncollected permits are 'released to the waitlist,' they may be given to someone else.
